methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Sicona Battery Technologies is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Christiaan Jordaan and Andrew Minett that has raised over US$22M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of nine private companies and one public equity in the battery and energy storage sector, with peer-median methodology applied across three same-stage, same-sector private companies.

The peer-median approach anchors the valuation at $150M, derived from three direct comparables: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M). This method captures the central tendency of Series A valuations in the climate and energy hardware space, establishing a range from $110M to $200M. With limited methodological diversity available in the current dataset, the peer-median result represents the primary triangulation point.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, with a central case of $150M. Key uncertainties include the limited number of directly comparable Series A stage peers in this capital-intensive sector and the absence of revenue or technology-specific metrics to differentiate Sicona within the peer set. Investors should note that this range reflects market comparables as of the analysis date and does not account for company-specific operational progress, intellectual property composition, or pathway to commercialization.
